description: Urgent re-review queue for the curmudgeon. Items here jump ALL normal cycle work (Phase 1, Phase 2, Phase 3). One item per curmudgeon run — guaranteed fresh context, no batch contamination. Throughput is managed by mode flag + decider scheduler mutation.

mode_rules.bau: Curmudgeon runs on normal 4h schedule. Priority queue is still honored — curmudgeon pops one item per run, but items drain at the normal cadence.
mode_rules.churn-and-burn: Decider, at end of every run, checks queue depth. If queue.length > 0, decider bumps curmudgeon schedule to every 30 min via update_scheduled_task. When queue empties, decider restores curmudgeon to every 4h AND auto-flips mode back to 'bau' (so a forgotten churn-and-burn can't leave curmudgeon pinned fast).

research_questions: (1) Do observable-consequence calculations in Part 1 (chromatic dispersion, critical angle at n=3.49, distance compression) change materially at n=2.84 vs 3.49?
(2) Does sun/firmament collision geometry shift with coefficient change?
(3) Do any of the 6 affected WINs (001, 016, 026, 064, 065, 069) lose or gain force?
(4) Can the coefficient change be cited as direct evidence of post-hoc parameter tuning? (Part 1 already says free coefficient 0.20 can accommodate any distance measurement.)
(5) Fix Part 1 values: currently cites table values (3.49, 28.8) not formula values — should cite both and flag the inconsistency.
